source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Pipeline Pump Fire at VK 900 A

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Viosca Knoll Block 900

actions_taken: Production operators shut down Pipeline Pump #2 and switched to Pipeline Pump #1
Operators smelled burning and identified smoke from exhaust piping insulation
Fire was immediately extinguished using nearby potable water hose
Pump was isolated after fire extinguishment
BSEE Accident Investigator conducted onsite investigation on 29 March 2021
event.summary: On 28 March 2021 at approximately 1115 hours, a fire occurred on Pipeline Pump #2 exhaust piping at Viosca Knoll Block 900 A platform. The fire was caused by corrosion-induced perforation in the exhaust muffler inlet piping, which allowed excessive heat to ignite the piping insulation blanket. The fire occurred in a Class 1 Division 2 area. Operators nearby immediately extinguished the fire using a potable water hose. No injuries, major damage, or pollution resulted. The incident was attributed to inadequate preventative maintenance.
recommendations: Implement enhanced preventative maintenance program for exhaust systems to prevent corrosion

controls.name: Preventative maintenance program for exhaust systems
controls.lod_basis: Routine inspection and maintenance to prevent equipment failure

supporting_text: Equipment failure – Inadequate preventative maintenance, excessive corrosion on the Pipeline Pump # 2's exhaust is the main contributor to the fire
Lessee failed to maintain equipment properly. Corrosion to the exhaust muffler located on Pipeline Pump #2 resulted in section loss

controls.name: Exhaust piping insulation
controls.barrier_role: Contain heat and prevent ignition of surrounding materials
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to isolate hot surfaces from flammable materials

supporting_text: The hole in the piping allowed heat to ignite the piping insulation
Corrosion weakened the wall of the muffler causing it to crack and introduce excessive temperatures to the insulation blanket. This caused the insulation to smolder and catch fire

controls.name: Operator detection and manual fire suppression
controls.barrier_role: Detect fire and suppress using available resources
controls.lod_basis: Human detection and immediate manual intervention to extinguish fire

supporting_text: two production operators in route to the Lease Automatic Custody Transfer (LACT) unit smelled something burning
They looked towards the pipeline pump area and saw smoke originating from the exhaust piping insulation
Using a nearby potable water hose, the operators were able to extinguish the flame quickly and safely
BSEE found that Cox's response to fire was adequate and followed regulations

controls.name: SCADA system for pump monitoring and control
controls.barrier_role: Monitor pump operation and enable remote shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory control system to manage pump operations and detect anomalies

supporting_text: production operators shut down Pipeline Pump #2 labeled PAX 1243 and switched to Pipeline Pump #1 for better volume and pressure using the Supervisory Control And Data Acquisition (SCADA)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
